{"author": null, "closed_at": null, "collaborating_teams": null, "collaborating_users": null, "created_at": null, "credits": [{"login": "SamyGhannad", "type": "reporter"}], "credits_detailed": [{"state": "accepted", "type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8165968?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/SamyGhannad/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/SamyGhannad/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/SamyGhannad/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/SamyGhannad/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/SamyGhannad", "id": 8165968, "login": "SamyGhannad", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjgxNjU5Njg=", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/SamyGhannad/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/SamyGhannad/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/SamyGhannad/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/SamyGhannad/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/SamyGhannad/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/SamyGhannad", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-44283", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": null, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}}, "cwe_ids": [], "cwes": [], "description": "### Impact
_What kind of vulnerability is it? Who is impacted?_

A vulnerability in etcd allows read access via PrevKv, or lease attachment in Put requests within transaction operations, to bypass RBAC authorization checks. An authenticated user without sufficient read or lease-related permissions may be able to access unauthorized data or attach leases by invoking transaction operations with these features enabled.

Kubernetes does not rely on etcd’s built-in authentication and authorization. Instead, the API server handles authentication and authorization itself, so typical Kubernetes deployments are not affected.

### Patches
_Has the problem been patched? What versions should users upgrade to?_

This vulnerability is patched in the following versions:
- etcd 3.6.11
- etcd 3.5.30
- etcd 3.4.44

### Workarounds
_Is there a way for users to fix or remediate the vulnerability without upgrading?_

If upgrading is not immediately possible, reduce exposure by treating the affected
RPCs as unauthenticated in practice.

- restrict network access to etcd server ports so only trusted components can connect
- require strong client identity at the transport layer, such as mTLS with tightly scoped client certificate
distribution

### Reporters

Samy Ghannad (@SamyGhannad on Github) reported that read access via PrevKv in a Put request within etcd transactions bypassed RBAC authorization checks. Benjamin Wang (@ahrtr ) further analyzed that lease attachment in a Put request within etcd transactions also bypassed RBAC authorization checks

### References
_Are there any links users can visit to find out more?_

title: RBAC authorization bypass in etcd allows unauthorized data access via PrevKv or lease attachment in Put requests nested in transactions
